title: Retail & E-Commerce
shortDescription: Multi-channel retail accounting, marketplace TCS/TDS compliance (Section 194-O), e-invoicing, and high-volume inventory reconciliation.
fullDescription: Retail networks and e-commerce platforms handle immense transactional volumes across online marketplaces (Amazon, Flipkart, Shopify) and physical retail chains. Anil Puneet and Associates provides multi-channel e-commerce tax compliance, marketplace TCS/TDS reconciliations, high-volume inventory audit, and automated GST e-invoicing solutions.
keyChallenges: Reconciling marketplace settlement reports against actual bank deposits and sales ledgers
Section 194-O TDS deducted by e-commerce operators vs. Form 26AS reflection
GST Section 52 Tax Collected at Source (TCS) reconciliation across multi-state registrations
Managing inventory shrinkage, return order accounting, and multi-warehouse stock audit
tailoredSolutions: Automated data reconciliation scripts comparing marketplace sales, returns, and TCS reports
Multi-state GST registration setup and principal place of business (APOB/PPOB) compliance
Real-time e-invoicing API integration for high-volume B2B e-commerce orders
Retail point-of-sale (POS) integration with core financial ledgers for automated audit trails

caseStudy.title: E-Commerce Marketplace TCS/TDS Reconciliation for Multi-Brand Retailer
caseStudy.context: An online consumer goods brand selling across 4 marketplaces faced significant discrepancies between sales registers, marketplace payout reports, and Section 194-O TDS credits.
caseStudy.approach: Our firm deployed structured monthly reconciliation templates matching channel sales, return credit notes, marketplace commissions, and Form 26AS TDS entries.
caseStudy.outcome: Recovered ₹12 Lakhs in unmatched TCS/TDS credits and eliminated monthly accounting delays.
